title: The TCP/IP Protocol Stack: A Systems Approach
description: A layered flowchart showing the four core layers of the TCP/IP model and how data flows from an application down through the network stack for transmission and back up for reception.
relationships: Data flows downward for encapsulation/transmission, starting at the Application Layer and gaining headers at each layer (e.g., TCP segment, IP packet, Ethernet frame).
Data flows upward for decapsulation/reception, where each layer processes and removes its respective header before passing the payload to the layer above.
